skill.name: azure-mgmt-applicationinsights-dotnet
skill.description: Guidance for using the Azure Resource Manager Application Insights SDK for .NET to create and manage monitoring resources.
skill.summary: Manage Azure Application Insights resources from .NET with ARM SDK examples.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found the high-severity cryptography and reconnaissance alerts are false positives, and most command alerts are Markdown or code examples. The main confirmed concern is sample code that prints connection strings, instrumentation keys, and an API key to console output.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Values Printed In Examples
medium_findings.description: The examples print Application Insights connection strings, instrumentation keys, and an API key. Copying these snippets into real tools could expose secrets through console logs or build logs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly writes secret-bearing values to console output. The content is instructional rather than malicious, so the severity is medium.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged As Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited areas are Markdown code fences, inline API names, tables, and examples. No skill file executes these snippets automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is visible documentation syntax in a Markdown file. There is no Ruby file, shell wrapper, or automatic execution path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Match
low_findings.description: The high static cryptography alerts do not correspond to cryptographic code. The cited lines contain the skill description and ordinary web test description text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines do not name or invoke weak algorithms such as MD5, SHA1, DES, or RC4. They are descriptive text for Azure monitoring examples.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation And Placeholder URLs
low_findings.description: The URL findings are Microsoft schema and documentation links plus example.com targets for sample web tests. No evidence found of credential exfiltration or hidden callbacks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The domains are official Microsoft, NuGet, GitHub, or example placeholders. The surrounding context describes availability tests and reference links.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Alert
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alert points to an error-handling example that prints an Azure validation message. It does not collect host details or enumerate the local system.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line only formats an exception message from Azure SDK sample code. No commands or APIs for local reconnaissance are present.

low_findings.title: Destructive Resource Operation Example
low_findings.description: The skill includes delete examples for an Application Insights component and web test. This is legitimate resource management guidance but can cause data loss if used without review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The destructive calls are explicit and easy to identify. They are expected SDK operations, so the concern is user safety rather than malicious intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Secret Output To Console
dangerous_patterns.description: Sample code writes secret-bearing Application Insights values to console output. Users should redact these values or route them to a secret store instead.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly visible in the examples. It is dangerous when copied into production automation or CI logs.

dangerous_patterns.title: Resource Deletion Calls
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation includes SDK calls that delete monitoring resources. These calls are legitimate but require explicit confirmation in generated guidance.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The delete calls are present and clear. They are not hidden, so this is a cautionary pattern rather than evidence of malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Azure monitoring setup can be verbose and easy to misconfigure. This skill provides focused .NET ARM SDK examples for Application Insights resources, web tests, workbooks, keys, and linked storage.
actual_capabilities: Shows package installation for Azure.ResourceManager.ApplicationInsights and Azure.Identity.
Provides examples for creating workspace-based Application Insights components.
Covers API keys, availability web tests, workbooks, and linked storage workflows.
Shows list, update, delete, and error-handling patterns for Application Insights resources.
Includes related SDK references and official documentation links.
limitations: This is documentation guidance and does not execute Azure operations by itself.
Examples require Azure subscription access and suitable Azure RBAC permissions.
Placeholders must be replaced with real resource names, IDs, and regions.
SDK versions and Azure API behavior should be checked against current Microsoft documentation.

use_cases.title: Create Monitoring Resources
use_cases.description: Generate ARM SDK code for a workspace-based Application Insights component with retention, sampling, tags, and Log Analytics linkage.

use_cases.title: Automate Availability Tests
use_cases.description: Build .NET examples for ping or multi-step web tests across Azure test locations.

use_cases.title: Prepare Observability Dashboards
use_cases.description: Create workbook and query examples that summarize request traffic and support operational review.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Basic Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to create a workspace-based Azure Application Insights component in .NET using Azure.ResourceManager.ApplicationInsights.

prompt_templates.title: Add Availability Testing
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a .NET ARM SDK example for an Application Insights web test that checks my homepage from three Azure locations.

prompt_templates.title: Build A Workbook
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design an Application Insights workbook for request count, failure rate, and response time trends using .NET resource management.

prompt_templates.title: Review Production Automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Application Insights provisioning plan for secure key handling, tagging, retention, sampling, private endpoints, and deletion safeguards.

output_examples.input: Request to create a workspace-based Application Insights component.
output_examples.output: A step-by-step .NET ARM SDK outline with required packages, authentication, component settings, tags, and creation flow.

output_examples.input: Request to add an availability test for a login flow.
output_examples.output: A structured plan for web test configuration, test locations, request sequence, timeout values, and secret handling notes.

output_examples.input: Request to improve an Application Insights automation script.
output_examples.output: A review checklist covering permissions, retention, sampling, logging hygiene, private endpoint options, and delete-operation safeguards.

best_practices: Use managed identity or DefaultAzureCredential for authentication whenever possible.
Store connection strings and API keys in a secret manager, not console logs.
Review delete operations and production tags before applying generated resource management code.
anti_patterns: Do not print Application Insights API keys or connection strings in logs.
Do not copy placeholder resource IDs without replacing subscription and resource group values.
Do not enable public network access without checking organizational security requirements.

faq.question: What Azure SDK does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It focuses on Azure.ResourceManager.ApplicationInsights for managing Application Insights resources from .NET.

faq.question: Can it create monitoring resources automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples that a developer can adapt and run in their own Azure environment.

faq.question: Does it support web tests?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes examples for ping tests, multi-step tests, test locations, and linked Application Insights resources.

faq.question: Does it cover workbooks?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows how to create shared workbooks with serialized dashboard content and KQL-based views.

faq.question: What permissions are required?
faq.answer: Users need Azure permissions to create, update, or delete Application Insights resources in the target resource group.

faq.question: What security caution applies?
faq.answer: Generated code should not print keys, connection strings, or other secret values to logs or console output.
